mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS levels (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack signals imminent corporate earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 70% of periods where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with accelerating consumer prices indicate demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when RSAFS and CPIAUCSL both increase, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production signal immediate supply-side contraction and reduced corporate output, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during post-shock recovery periods, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 levels show zero correlation with 60-day forward returns, and the hypothesis yields a negative Sharpe ratio in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above 0.4% monthly triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in 65% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold, invalidating the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate indicate a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending and corporate profits, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in majority of triggered cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Surges in housing starts signal expanding residential investment and durable goods demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during economic expansions.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 60-day returns during mid-cycle expansions, with directional accuracy dropping below 40% in out-of-sample periods.
